Requirement Text: CAN Driver shall support Standard and Extended Identifier.The CAN driver shall be able to operate with both standard and extended CAN Identifiers on one CAN Controller if supported by CAN Hardware.

CAN Driver shall allow static configuration of hardware reception filter.HW supported filtering of receive L-PDUs shall be configurable during initialization phase; reconfiguration during normal operation shall only be possible in STOPPED mode.

Bit timing of CAN Controller shall be configurable.The Baud Rate of each CAN controller served by the CAN Driver shall be configurable, including propagation delay, Tseg1, Tseg2, Samples/bit, and SJW.
Hardware Object Handles shall be provided for the CAN Interface in the static configuration file. All available hardware object handles shall be defined in the ECU configuration description.

It shall be configurable whether Multiplex Transmission is used. The Multiplexed Transmission feature shall be Pre-Compile-Time configurable and only supported if the underlying CAN Controller supports it.

Each event for each CAN Controller shall be configurable to be detected by polling or by an interrupt. Each possible event shall be Pre-Compile-Time configurable to operate in either Polling or Interrupt-driven mode
It shall be possible to configure one or several TX Hardware Objects, where each Hardware Object is represented by its own Hardware Object Handle.

The CAN Driver shall implement an interface for initialization. This service shall initialize all module global variables and all Registers of the CAN Hardware Unit and its Controllers. This function shall only be called once during startup.

The CAN Driver shall support dynamic selection of configuration sets. The driver shall support selection of one static configuration set from a list, passed via the initialization interface; switching is only possible in STOPPED mode.

The CAN Driver shall provide a service to enable/disable interrupts of the CAN Controller. Disabling means disabling all interrupts; enabling means re-enabling all interrupts that were disabled before.
The CAN Driver shall guarantee data consistency of received L-PDUs. The driver shall guarantee that the data inside a Hardware Object is not overwritten while it is copied.

The CAN Driver shall offer a reception indication service. The CAN Driver shall notify the CAN Interface about a successful reception via a static callback function, including CAN Identifier, DLC, Hardware Object, and pointer to SDU data.
The CAN Driver shall provide a dynamic transmission request service. The API shall accept CAN Hardware Object Handle, pointer to L-SDU source, CAN Identifier, and DLC as parameters.

The CAN Driver shall provide a transmission confirmation service. The driver shall notify the CAN Interface about a successful transmission (at least one receiver acknowledged and no error disturbed the frame) via a static callback function.
The CAN Driver shall provide a service to change the CAN controller mode, supporting states: UNINIT, STOPPED, STARTED, and SLEEP.
The CAN Driver shall provide a notification for controller wakeup events. The driver shall notify the Service Layer in case of a wake-up interrupt; this feature is Pre-Compile-Time configurable.

The CAN driver shall support the situation where a wakeup by bus occurs during the same time the transition to standby/sleep is in progress. The driver shall raise the Wake-up Notification immediately after the standby/sleep API has finished.

The CAN driver shall be able to detect notification events message object specific by CAN-Interrupt and polling. Both mechanisms shall be configurable for each message object if supported by CAN Hardware.
The CAN Driver shall support multiplexed transmission (three TX HW objects represented as one Hardware Object Handle) if supported by the underlying CAN Controller, to avoid outer priority inversion.

The CAN Driver shall not support remote frames. The CAN driver shall not transmit messages triggered by remote transmission requests and shall initialize the CAN HW to ignore any remote transmission requests.

The CAN driver shall be able to operate with both classic CAN and CAN FD frames on one CAN Controller if supported by CAN Hardware. CAN FD frames support up to 64 bytes per frame at a higher baud rate.
The CAN Driver shall support CAN XL besides CAN 2.0 and CAN FD. CAN XL provides higher bandwidth and allows native tunneling of Ethernet frames.
The CAN Driver shall provide a function to return the current CAN controller error state (ACTIVE, PASSIVE, BUSOFF).
The CAN Driver shall provide a function to return the current CAN controller Rx and Tx error counters via dedicated functions.
The CAN Driver shall provide a service to fetch the message state of an L-PDU. (Status: DRAFT) L-PDUs encapsulated with IEEE1722 protocol require the message state added to the ACF header.
The CAN Driver shall implement an interface for de-initialization. This service shall de-initialize the CAN Hardware Unit and its Controllers to allow re-configuration without an ECU reset.
CAN Driver shall provide a notification for bus-off state. The driver shall notify the CAN Interface if the CAN Controller goes in bus-off state via a static callback function.
The CAN driver shall not recover from bus-off automatically. Bus-off recovery shall be software driven; automatic hardware recovery must be suppressed by software.
CAN Interface shall import communication matrix
CAN Interface shall access driver configuration
The CAN Driver shall fulfill the general requirements for Basic Software Modules as specified in AUTOSAR_SRS_SPAL.
The CAN Driver shall fulfill the general requirements for Basic Software Modules as specified in AUTOSAR_SRS_SPAL.
The CAN Driver shall support multiple CAN controllers of the same CAN hardware unit. It shall be possible Pre-Compile-Time to de-select an unused CAN Controller.

The CAN Interface implementation and interface shall be independent from the underlying CAN Controller and CAN Transceiver. The Hardware Abstraction Layer encapsulates different mechanisms of hardware access.

The CAN State Manager shall offer a network abstract API to the upper layer (ComM). The CAN State Manager handles states of peripherals assigned to a network, performing Init, Start, Stop, WakeUp, Sleep, and BusOff Recovery actions.
The CAN State Manager shall offer a network configuration independent interface for upper layers. The interface to upper layers shall be independent from the network configuration.
The CAN stack shall ensure not to lose messages in receive direction. The HW receive buffer shall be read out in a time frame that no message is lost for a bus load of 100% with a payload of 1 byte.
The CAN stack shall be able to produce 100% bus load (except gaps resulting from not using multiplexed HW transmit buffers), using CAN frames with 1 byte payload.
